subject: Piano
audience: Students learning their first songs and building practice grit.

units.name: Rhythm River
units.metaphor: A steady current that keeps every song moving.
units.description: Pulse, counting, note values, and staying steady under pressure.

units.name: Keyboard Village
units.metaphor: A familiar neighborhood of notes and patterns.
units.description: Note names, hand position, intervals, and keyboard geography.

units.name: Coordination Bridge
units.metaphor: Where both hands learn to move together without panic.
units.description: Hand independence, simple chords, and slow-loop repair.

concepts.name: Steady Pulse
concepts.description: Keeping a consistent beat while playing or resting.
concepts.story: Pulse is the ground under the music. When it holds, everything else can stand.
concepts.grit_prompt: If the beat wobbles, slow down. A steady comeback counts more than a fast mistake.
concepts.visual_metaphor: A glowing metronome river with evenly spaced stepping stones.

ingredients.label: Counting beats aloud
ingredients.description: Saying the beat while playing so timing becomes visible.
ingredients.failure_mode: Student plays by feel and loses the beat when notes get harder.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Clap and count four slow measures before touching the keys.
ingredients.visual_metaphor: Four stones lighting up one at a time.

ingredients.label: Tempo control
ingredients.description: Keeping the same speed from beginning to end.
ingredients.failure_mode: Student speeds up easy parts and rushes through hard parts.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Play the same measure three times at 60 BPM without speeding up.
ingredients.visual_metaphor: A metronome beam that stays the same length.

concepts.name: Keyboard Geography
concepts.description: Finding notes by recognizing black-key groups and intervals.
concepts.story: The keyboard stops being a blur when landmarks appear.
concepts.grit_prompt: Getting lost is normal. Find the two-black-key group and rebuild your map.
concepts.visual_metaphor: A village map built around groups of two and three black keys.

ingredients.label: Black-key landmarks
ingredients.description: Using groups of two and three black keys to locate white-key notes.
ingredients.failure_mode: Student memorizes isolated notes and cannot recover after getting lost.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Find every C on the keyboard using only the two-black-key group.

concepts.name: Left-Hand Independence
concepts.description: Keeping a simple left-hand pattern stable while the right hand moves.
concepts.story: Independence begins when one hand can stay calm while the other explores.
concepts.grit_prompt: Do not fight both hands at once. Stabilize one hand, then invite the other back in.
concepts.visual_metaphor: Two bridges crossing the same river at different rhythms.

ingredients.label: Left-hand loop
ingredients.description: Repeating a short bass or chord pattern without drifting.
ingredients.failure_mode: Student changes the left hand whenever the right hand becomes demanding.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Loop the left hand alone until it feels boring, then add one right-hand note.
ingredients.visual_metaphor: A looped path glowing under the left hand.
